text: Git branching is an essential part of version control. It allows developers to work on new features without affecting the main codebase. A common workflow involves creating a feature branch from the main branch. Here, you can make changes, test them, and commit your code safely. Once the feature is complete and stable, you merge it back into the main branch. In team projects, this is often done through a pull request. A pull request lets others review your changes before merging, ensuring quality and avoiding conflicts. This structured workflow helps teams collaborate smoothly and keeps the project history clean and organized.
